avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack is slowly consolidating, however instabilities will still exist mainly on West through North to East aspects above 850 metres. Areas most affected will be steep terrain such as gullies and coire headwalls. Cornices will be unstable and liable to collapse. The snowpack will be thawing and there will be significant snow loss, particularly at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Showers overnight, falling as snow only on the highest summits. A drier morning will follow before the onset of persistent rain at all levels, as the freezing level rises to well above the summits. Very strong winds through Friday night, decreasing to become strong, generally from a South-Westerly direction.
Observed Weather Influences: There was fresh snow falling down to 650 metres overnight, but as the freezing level rose during the day, precipitation quickly become rain at all levels apart from the highest summits. Strong South-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh windslab had developed overnight and this was becoming increasingly unstable with the onset of rain. Areas most affected are steep North-West through North to East aspects above 900m. Cornices are prone to collapse. The snowpack is thawing with noticeable snow loss at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to thaw in all locations. Instability\'s will persist within deeper accumulations found predominately on West through North to North-East aspects above 800 metres. Isolated areas also will also be on South-East and East aspects and at lower altitudes.  Greatest hazard will be found on steep terrain such as scarp slopes, gullies and steep burn lines. Many of these areas have cornices above them which will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The thaw continues with the freezing level well above the summits and occasional rain. Winds backing from the West to become fresh South-South-Westerlies for most of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Precipitation fell as snow overnight above 500 metres before turning to rain. The freezing level was around 900 metres by midday. Winds veered and strengthen throughout the day becoming gale force West-South-Westerlies this afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: As the freezing level rose and falling snow turned to rain the snowpack started to thaw. By lunchtime the old snow-ice had started to soften with recent snowfall becoming soft and moist. Weaknesses persist within deeper accumulations mainly on West through North to North-East aspects above 900 metres. Cornices will be prone to collapse above these locations.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Despite some consolidation of the snow pack wet snow instabilities will be present particularly with the onset of heavy rain. Steep North-West through North to East aspects above 950 metres will be most affected. Cornices above these aspects will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Showers overnight will be rain or wet summit snow followed by heavy rain at all levels from late morning. The freezing level will be around 1100 metres overnight rising above all summits during the day. Strong South-Westerly winds.
Observed Weather Influences: Precipitation gradually turned to rain at higher levels alternating between rain and wet snow above around 1000 metres during the afternoon. The freezing level rose to around 1100 metres with very strong South-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Further windslab development overnight with wet snow instabilities developing during the day. Steep North-West through North to East aspects above 900 metres are most affected. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to thaw. There will be some slight stabilisation overnight. However, wet snow instabilities will remain, these mainly on North to East aspects above 900 metres. Some substantial cornices exist above these aspects, and these will remain prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A few showers of rain or wet snow are expected overnight. The freezing level will be about 1200 metres. During the day the freezing level will rise well above the summits and rain will fall at all levels.
Observed Weather Influences: There were periods of heavy precipitation overnight and through the day. Overnight it fell as snow above about 800 metres,. This rose to about 1050 metres during the day as the freezing level rose to around 1250 metres. Strong winds were from the South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: Overnight areas of fresh windslab were deposited on mainly North to East aspects above 900 metres. This was going through a period of instability as the freezing level rose and the snowpack became wet. Cornices above these aspects are prone to collapse. Stability on other aspects was better. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will continue to consolidate and thaw in the rain and warmer temperatures. There will be some snow loss, through Saturday afternoon, in the heavier rain. Greatest accumulations will remain above 800 metres, on West, through North and East to South aspects.
The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: There will be patchy rain overnight and through the morning, to all levels, with more persistent rain during the afternoon.  Winds will be very strong, South-Westerly throughout the period, with a slight lull during Saturday morning to fresh. Freezing level will initially be just above the summits, but will rise to well above the summits by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: It has been cloudy with rain to all levels with a little sleet and hail at the highest elevations. Winds were strong to very strong, South-Westerly, with the freezing level above the summits since mid morning.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is thawing and consolidating at all levels. Largest accumulations are above 700 metres, on West, through North and East to South aspects. Although softening, there are still firm. and icy sections at higher elevations. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to thaw below 1100 metres then later at all levels. Instabilities will remain within the snowpack in steep and convex terrain and be most widespread above 900 metres on North-West to North-East aspects, more localised on West and East. On these aspects between 500 and 900 metres localised instabilities will persist on steep banks and burn lines. Cornices will be prone to collapse, potentially triggering slopes below. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force Westerly winds will ease to strong during the day and back South-Westerly. Light precipitation expected over night and during the afternoon.  Initially this will fall as snow above 1100 metres before turning to rain at all levels during the afternoon. The 1100 metre freezing level will rise to above the summits by dawn.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale force South-Westerly winds with precipitation falling as light snow above 1100 metres. The freezing level rose to the summits by midday
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack surface is beginning to thaw at all but the highest levels. Instabilities persist on North-West to North-East aspects, these are most widespread on steep terrain above 800 metres and more localised on West and East aspects. Debris from recent avalanche activity was noted on a steep Northerly aspect at around 1000 metres. Between 500 and 800 metres localised instabilities remain on steep and convex banks and burn lines. Cornices are unstable. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
